canonical_definition: Across the surveyed literature, Usability is characterized in several distinct ways, often focusing on how well a system serves its intended users in specific contexts. A prevalent framing, termed "User-centricity," defines it as a quality that prioritizes the user's experience, specifically regarding "the interpretability of its results and the user's ability to interact with and control the process." This perspective is reinforced by the construct's stated relationship with both `Interpretability and explainability` and `Interactivity`, with one paper noting that single-turn systems suffer from limited user-centricity. Other definitions present a more application-specific view, such as its role in enabling effective use in "real documentation workflows." A specialized variant, "Patient-centricity," adapts the concept to healthcare, measuring the degree to which model responses prioritize a patient's individual needs and context. To operationalize this construct, papers report using `G-Eval` for measurement. In the healthcare context, it is also evaluated as a metric alongside fluency, empathy, and ROUGE-L scores.
papers: A Training-Free Length Extrapolation Approach forLLMs: Greedy Attention Logit Interpolation
Back Attention: Understanding and Enhancing Multi-Hop Reasoning in Large Language Models
Dynamic Collaboration of Multi-Language Models based on Minimal Complete Semantic Units

definitions.text: The degree to which a system can be effectively used in real documentation workflows by intended users.
definitions.paper: Back Attention: Understanding and Enhancing Multi-Hop Reasoning in Large Language Models

definitions.text: The quality of an AI system that prioritizes the user's experience, specifically in terms of the interpretability of its results and the user's ability to interact with and control the process.
definitions.paper: A Training-Free Length Extrapolation Approach forLLMs: Greedy Attention Logit Interpolation

definitions.text: The degree to which the model's responses prioritize the patient's individual needs, beliefs, and behavioral context in shaping health-related guidance and communication.
